title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Spiral Review
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Compiler Performance evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Compiler Performance assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A review build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 2. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.

content.text: Order work so Compiler Performance has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
